title: Effectiveness of daratumumab plus bortezomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone (DVRd) versus VRd for transplant-eligible newly diagnosed multiple myeloma.
abstract: Frontline (1L) treatment for transplant-eligible (TE) newly diagnosed multiple myeloma (NDMM) has shifted from triplet (e.g. bortezomib [V], lenalidomide [R], dexamethasone [d]) to quadruplet (e.g. daratumumab with VRd [DVRd]) regimens, based on improved efficacy demonstrated in pivotal trials. This real-world study compared progression-free survival (PFS) in TE patients with NDMM treated with DVRd plus DR or R maintenance (DVRd-DR/R) versus VRd plus R maintenance (VRd-R).
trial_name: Daratumumab, bortezomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone for Multiple Myeloma
